name: deck-label
text: EyeReply / VisioALS

name: cover-title
text: Communication should still sound like you.
textPreview: Communication should still sound like you.

name: cover-subtitle
text: Webcam gaze AAC with personalized candidate replies
textPreview: Webcam gaze AAC with personalized candidate replies

name: cover-byline
text: Kevin Xia  •  ICCHP 2026 Innovation Area

Open with one sentence: ‘AAC can restore a channel for speech, but a channel is not the same thing as authorship.’ Then introduce EyeReply, the name used in the ICCHP program, and VisioALS, the software name. Do not start with the tech stack.

title: A correct reply can still feel like the wrong person

name: title-2
text: A correct reply can still feel like the wrong person
textPreview: A correct reply can still feel like the wrong person

name: example-context
text: Illustrative example — same intent, different linguistic identity
textPreview: Illustrative example — same intent, different linguistic identity

name: question
text: “Want some tea?”

name: generic-label
text: GENERIC

name: generic-quote
text: “Yes, please.”

name: identity-label
text: STYLE-MATCHED CANDIDATE

name: identity-quote
text: “Absolutely. Tea is doing most of the work today.”
textPreview: “Absolutely. Tea is doing most of the work today.”

name: identity-takeaway
text: The goal is not more eloquent text. It is recognizable authorship.
textPreview: The goal is not more eloquent text. It is recognizable authorship.

text: This example is intentionally labeled illustrative; do not present it as user-study evidence. Say: ‘The innovation I care about is not simply generating a plausible answer. It is generating choices the person would plausibly choose and phrase themselves.’

name: title-3
text: The interaction is deliberately simple

name: flow-1
text: 1  A partner speaks

name: flow-2
text: 2  Four candidate replies appear

name: flow-3
text: 3  The user selects by gaze

name: flow-4
text: 4  The chosen reply is spoken

name: reject-callout
text: “None of these” is always an answer.
textPreview: “None of these” is always an answer.

name: demo-screenshot
alt: VisioALS interface showing four response choices and a None of these option

text: Use this slide only to set up the live demo. Ask someone in the room to say, ‘How are you doing today?’ Then let the system run. Narrate as little as possible while it is working. Deliberately point out ‘None of these’ because rejection is part of user agency, not an error state.

title: “Sounds like me” has three separate layers

name: title-4
text: “Sounds like me” has three separate layers
textPreview: “Sounds like me” has three separate layers

name: stage-title-1
text: Learn the style

name: stage-body-1
text: Past writing, plus transcripts from audio or video, describe vocabulary, brevity, tone and recurring expressions.
textPreview: Past writing, plus transcripts from audio or video, describe vocabulary, brevity, tone and recurring expressions.

name: stage-title-2
text: Retrieve what is relevant

name: stage-body-2
text: Similar past snippets help shape wording. They are style evidence—not facts about what the person wants now.
textPreview: Similar past snippets help shape wording. They are style evidence—not facts about what the person wants now.

name: stage-title-3
text: Adapt through choices

name: stage-body-3
text: Selections and rejections become preference rules. The language can change; the current intent still comes from the user.
textPreview: Selections and rejections become preference rules. The language can change; the current intent still comes from the user.

text: Be precise here. Voice cloning is how the output sounds acoustically; linguistic identity is how the reply is worded. They are different. Also emphasize the safety boundary implemented in the prompt: profile material controls style only, never subject matter.

title: The user must remain the author

name: title-5
text: The user must remain the author
textPreview: The user must remain the author

name: agency-label-1
text: REJECT

name: agency-title-1
text: None of these

name: agency-body-1
text: Rejection generates a new set instead of forcing a bad choice.
textPreview: Rejection generates a new set instead of forcing a bad choice.

name: agency-label-2
text: ACCESS

name: agency-title-2
text: Eyes or head

name: agency-body-2
text: The input mode can follow changing motor ability.
textPreview: The input mode can follow changing motor ability.

name: agency-label-3
text: FALLBACK

name: agency-title-3
text: Communication continues

name: agency-body-3
text: Local speech recognition and system TTS reduce total dependence on cloud services.
textPreview: Local speech recognition and system TTS reduce total dependence on cloud services.

name: agency-label-4
text: LEARNING

name: agency-title-4
text: Choices teach the system

name: agency-body-4
text: Adaptation is based on what the person selects—not what the model assumes.
textPreview: Adaptation is based on what the person selects—not what the model assumes.

text: This is the design-principles slide. Keep it human-centered. Avoid listing model names unless someone asks. The strongest point is that generative suggestions do not remove authorship if the person retains choice, rejection and adaptation control.

title: We should evaluate the interaction, not just the model

name: title-6
text: We should evaluate the interaction, not just the model
textPreview: We should evaluate the interaction, not just the model

name: evidence-intro
text: Three small, honest measurements are enough for an Innovation Area demo.
textPreview: Three small, honest measurements are enough for an Innovation Area demo.

name: measure-title-1
text: Selection

name: measure-body-1
text:
Prompted looks to each target

Report target hit rate and recalibrations.
textPreview: Prompted looks to each target |  | Report target hit rate and recalibrations.

name: measure-title-2
text: Speed

name: measure-body-2
text:
Question end → speech start

Report median and slowest trial.
textPreview: Question end → speech start |  | Report median and slowest trial.

name: measure-title-3
text: Identity

name: measure-body-3
text:
Blind A/B: generic vs personalized

Ask: “Which sounds more like you?”
textPreview: Blind A/B: generic vs personalized |  | Ask: “Which sounds more like you?”

name: evidence-footer
text: Prototype evidence ≠ clinical evidence. Label the sample and conditions.
textPreview: Prototype evidence ≠ clinical evidence. Label the sample and conditions.

text: If you can collect these before presenting, replace the descriptions with real numbers and state the device, lighting, number of trials and participant count. If you cannot, present this as the evaluation plan and say so. Do not call automated unit tests a user benchmark.

title: This is a working prototype—and real-world validation is next

name: title-7
text: This is a working prototype—and real-world validation is next
textPreview: This is a working prototype—and real-world validation is next

name: works-title
text: What works now

name: works-body
text:
• Standard webcam
• Short calibration
• Four gaze-selectable replies
• Eye/head input modes
• Personalized wording and voice
textPreview: • Standard webcam | • Short calibration | • Four gaze-selectable replies | • Eye/head input modes | • Personalized wording and voice

name: needs-title
text: What still needs evidence

name: needs-body
text: Use by people with ALS, across fatigue, posture, lighting and real conversations.
textPreview: Use by people with ALS, across fatigue, posture, lighting and real conversations.

name: calibration-screenshot
alt: VisioALS moving-dot webcam gaze calibration screen

text: This slide buys credibility. Say clearly whether any person with ALS has tested the current version. Experts at ICCHP will care more about an honest boundary and a sensible next study than an inflated claim. Invite collaborators who can help with participatory evaluation.

name: close-label
text: The question

name: close-title
text: Can fast AAC preserve not only speech—but authorship?
textPreview: Can fast AAC preserve not only speech—but authorship?

name: close-cta
text: Try it. Reject a reply. Tell me where it stops feeling like the user.
textPreview: Try it. Reject a reply. Tell me where it stops feeling like the user.

name: close-footer
text: EyeReply / VisioALS  •  open source

Close by returning to the opening distinction between channel and authorship. Then move immediately to hands-on interaction: ‘Please try it. I am especially interested in the failures—bad suggestions, awkward gaze behavior, or anything that feels presumptuous.’
